description:
You are assisting a database administrator who wants a quick, text–based overview of key PostgreSQL tuning parameters that live inside a single INI-style configuration file.  
The administrator has placed the file

    /home/user/db_configs/db_service.ini

on disk.  Your goals are:

1. Parse that INI file (do not hard-code values; read them from the file).  
2. Create a **new** directory

       /home/user/query_tuning

   if it does not already exist.  
3. Produce the text file

       /home/user/query_tuning/optimization_summary.log

   that contains exactly **seven** lines, one key/value pair per line, in the precise order and letter-case shown below:

       DATABASE=<value>
       HOST=<value>
       PORT=<value>
       MAX_CONNECTIONS=<value>
       WORK_MEM=<value>
       SHARED_BUFFERS=<value>
       EFFECTIVE_CACHE_SIZE=<value>

   • There must be no additional spaces before or after the equal sign.  
   • The file must end with a single trailing newline (i.e., a newline after the seventh line).  
   • No extra blank lines are allowed.  

Hints (not commands):  
• Under the [database] section you will find the keys host, port, dbname.  
• Under the [optimizations] section you will find the relevant performance-tuning keys.  
• Basic command-line text processing utilities (e.g., grep, awk, sed, cut) are sufficient; use whichever you prefer.  

When finished, the administrator should be able to run:

    cat /home/user/query_tuning/optimization_summary.log

and see the exact seven-line report described above.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent begins:

1. Directory: /home/user/db_configs/   (chmod 755)
2. File: /home/user/db_configs/db_service.ini (chmod 644) with the following exact byte contents:

[database]
host=localhost
port=5432
dbname=inventory
user=inventory_admin

[optimizations]
max_connections=200
work_mem=4MB
shared_buffers=128MB
effective_cache_size=512MB


Expected state after successful task completion:

1. Directory /home/user/query_tuning/ must exist (chmod 755).  
2. File /home/user/query_tuning/optimization_summary.log (chmod 644) must exist and contain exactly:

DATABASE=inventory
HOST=localhost
PORT=5432
MAX_CONNECTIONS=200
WORK_MEM=4MB
SHARED_BUFFERS=128MB
EFFECTIVE_CACHE_SIZE=512MB

<newline>

(There is a single ‘\n’ character after “EFFECTIVE_CACHE_SIZE=512MB”).  

Nothing else in the filesystem needs to be changed or created.
